assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both additive and reductive signals detected in test changes (see details below) |'. The detailed section (lines 133-177) explicitly identifies both additive and reductive signals across the test file changes.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 138-139 in the structural summary table: '| `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs` | modified | ... | -1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` removed) ...' and lines 143-144: 'Removed test function `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`: This function existed in the base-branch version (lines 30-48 of `test-base-purl-recommend.md`) and is entirely absent from the PR version.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 146-157: 'Relaxed assertion in `test_recommend_purls_basic`: In the base-branch version, the assertion checked for a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers: `pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar` In the PR version, this was changed to check a versioned PURL without qualifiers: `pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12`'. Also captured in the structural summary table line 139: '-1 assertion relaxed (fully qualified PURL check changed to versioned-only check)'.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 140 in the structural summary table: '| `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs` | new | +3 test functions, +9 assertions | (none -- new file) |'. Lines 161-162: 'New test file `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs` with 3 new test functions (`test_simplified_purl_no_version`, `test_simplified_purl_mixed_types`, `test_simplified_purl_ordering_preserved`) containing 9+ new assertions.'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 159-160: 'New test function `test_recommend_purls_dedup` added to `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs`: Tests deduplication behavior when qualifiers are stripped, asserting only 1 result is returned for 2 seeded PURLs with different qualifiers. This function and its 3 assertions did not exist in the base-branch version.' Also in structural summary table line 139: '+1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. These are all analysis/report documents. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report presents findings and verdicts but does not describe modifying any source files. The report content is purely analytical (scope containment, acceptance criteria evaluation, test classification).

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 17: 'Overall: PASS' followed by line 19: 'All checks pass. The Test Change Classification is MIXED (informational, does not affect overall verdict) due to the combination of new test additions and removal/relaxation of existing tests.' The report presents a verdict and detailed findings for human review. There is no merge action, no gh pr merge command, and no indication of auto-merging anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 96-98: The Test Change Classification section appears under '### Style/Conventions' heading. The Verdict Source Mapping table at lines 193-194 explicitly states: '| Test Change Classification | Style/Conventions sub-agent |' and '| Test Quality | Style/Conventions sub-agent (Repetitive Test Detection + Test Documentation combined) |'.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 133-134: 'Classification is based on file content comparison between the base-branch version (from `test-base-purl-recommend.md`) and the PR version (from `pr-diff-test-changes.md`), not on acceptance criteria or task requirements.' The structural summary and reductive/additive signals sections reference specific test function names, assertion code, and line numbers from the test files. For example, lines 143-144 reference 'lines 30-48 of `test-base-purl-recommend.md`' and lines 146-157 show actual assertion code from base-branch and PR-branch versions. The semantic assessment (lines 168-175) discusses coverage and assertion specificity in terms of test file content.
